lede: A look at July 19 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Dani Muñoz and Ryan O'Byrne, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Knesset
events.desc: The Knesset passes the controversial Nationality Bill, which defines the State of Israel as the nation-state of the Jewish people.

events.title: The Troubles
events.desc: The Troubles: The Provisional Irish Republican Army resumes a ceasefire to end their 25-year paramilitary campaign to end British rule in Northern Ireland.

events.title: Via D'Amelio bombing
events.desc: A car bomb kills Judge Paolo Borsellino and five members of his escort.

events.title: Piedmont Airlines Flight 22
events.desc: Piedmont Airlines Flight 22, a Piedmont Airlines Boeing 727-22 and a twin-engine Cessna 310 collided over Hendersonville, North Carolina, USA. Both aircraft were destroyed and all passengers and crew were killed, including John T. McNaughton, an advisor to Robert McNamara.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Battle of Fromelles: British and Australian troops attack German trenches as part of the Battle of the Somme.

events.title: Maurice Garin
events.desc: Maurice Garin wins the first Tour de France.

events.title: Taiping Rebellion
events.desc: Taiping Rebellion: Third Battle of Nanking: The Qing dynasty finally defeats the Taiping Heavenly Kingdom.

events.title: Great New York City Fire of 1845
events.desc: Great New York City Fire of 1845: The last great fire to affect Manhattan begins early in the morning and is subdued that afternoon. The fire kills four firefighters and 26 civilians and destroys 345 buildings.

events.title: Great Northern War
events.desc: Great Northern War: A numerically superior Polish-Saxon army of Augustus II the Strong, operating from an advantageous defensive position, is defeated by a Swedish army half its size under the command of King Charles XII in the Battle of Klissow.

events.title: Arab–Byzantine wars
events.desc: Arab–Byzantine wars: Battle of Apamea: Fatimids defeat a Byzantine army near Apamea.

births.name: Choi Yoon-chil
births.line: South Korean long-distance runner and a two-time national champion in the marathon (died 2020)

births.name: Xenophon Stratigos
births.line: Greek general and politician, Greek Minister of Transport (died 1927)

deaths.text: Paul M. Fleiss, American pediatrician and author
Agustín de Iturbide, Mexican general and emperor

observances.text: Christian feast day: Bernold, Bishop of Utrecht
Christian feast day: Macrina the Younger, Sister of St. Basil the Great
